---
title: "Data used in this work"
editor: visual
---

::::::::: columns
::: {.column width="49%"}
## Real Estate Data

<details>

<summary><strong style="font-size: 1.25em;">LIFFULL HOMES Listings</strong></summary>

LIFFUL'HOMES is one of the major real estate companies in Japan. The dataset includes housing listings with extremely high quality details.

-   Website: https://lifull.com/en/
-   70GB

</details>

<details>

<summary><strong style="font-size: 1.25em;">MLIT Land Prices</strong></summary>

-   Website: https://www.mlit.go.jp/en/
-   ?GB

</details>
:::

::: {.column width="2%"}
:::

::: {.column width="49%"}
## Risk Mapping Data

<details>

<summary><strong style="font-size: 1.25em;">JMA Earthquake Records</strong></summary>

Japan Meteorological Agency.

-   Website: https://www.jma.go.jp/jma/indexe.html

</details>

<details>

<summary><strong style="font-size: 1.25em;">J-SHIS Hazard Maps</strong></summary>

*Japan Seismic Hazard Information Station* (https://www.j-shis.bosai.go.jp/en/).

Many maps are available, easy to access and well described. I decide to fo for the so-called "Seismic hazard map." From there, I select "All", meaning I include earthquakes of all categories from I to III. I finally choose the "Average Case" as Probability Case. See download page for more information : <https://www.j-shis.bosai.go.jp/map/JSHIS2/download.html?lang=en>.

</details>
:::

::: {.column width="49%"}
## Panel Data

<details>

<summary><strong style="font-size: 1.25em;">KSPS Panel Data</strong></summary>

Large Panel from Keio University from 2004.

-   Website: https://www.pdrc.keio.ac.jp/en/paneldata/datasets/jhpskhps/

</details>

<details>

<summary><strong style="font-size: 1.25em;">GEES Data on earthquakes</strong></summary>

Panel after the 2011 earthquake about what Japanese people think about earthquakes.

-   Website: https://www.pdrc.keio.ac.jp/en/paneldata/datasets/gees/

</details>
:::

::: {.column width="2%"}
:::

::: {.column width="49%"}
## Other Useful Data

<details>

<summary><strong style="font-size: 1.25em;">E-Stat</strong></summary>

Unemployment data, everything else that could have an impact on prices.

</details>
:::
:::::::::

::: {style="margin-top: 40px;"}
:::

![](images/shm_2020_style.png)

## Data availability

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| error: false
#| eval: false
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

data <- data.frame(
  categorie = c("Risk Mapping", "Panel", "Housing Prices", "Land Prices", "Earthquakes"),
  debut = as.Date(c("2008-01-01", "2004-01-01", "2015-01-01", "2003-01-01", "2000-01-01")),
  fin   = as.Date(c("2025-01-01", "2024-01-01", "2017-01-01", "2026-01-01", "2026-01-01"))
)

data$categorie <- factor(data$categorie, levels = rev(data$categorie))

ggplot(data) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = debut, xend = fin,
                   y = categorie, yend = categorie),
               size = 4, color = "steelblue") +
  
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "5 years", date_labels = "%Y") +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL) +
  
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

![](images/data.png)
